payload.summary: This week's review pulse highlights significant concerns with INDmoney, including technical issues, misleading financial information, and poor customer support. Many users have reported problems with the app's functionality, such as difficulties with stock selling and buying, and discrepancies in cashback policies. Some users have also reported feeling misled by the app's policies and promotional messages.
quotes: Too many financial personal data taken very discreetly.. Also the Returns shown for all stocks etf are Absolute. Industry norm is showing CAGR.
I sold one stock, even though I sold it remains showing that stock in my portfolio, I asked help for this issue but not get answer for this glitch or error in my portfolio.
ALERT!! I'm Using INDMoney since 2024 Dont trust this application especially in case of US Stocks simply THIEF.
action_items: Improve the app's technical functionality to prevent glitches and errors, such as issues with stock selling and buying.
Enhance transparency in financial information, including clearly labeling absolute and CAGR returns to prevent user confusion.
Revise customer support policies to provide more direct and timely assistance to users, including phone support and clearer communication channels.
action_ideas: Improve the app's technical functionality to prevent glitches and errors, such as issues with stock selling and buying.
Enhance transparency in financial information, including clearly labeling absolute and CAGR returns to prevent user confusion.
Revise customer support policies to provide more direct and timely assistance to users, including phone support and clearer communication channels.

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem or withdraw their units from a mutual fund scheme.
The exit load is typically a percentage of the withdrawal amount or the net asset value (NAV) of the units being redeemed.
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly, thereby stabilizing the fund's assets and reducing the impact on remaining investors.
The exit load percentage and the time period for which it is applicable vary across different mutual fund schemes.
The exit load is usually deducted from the redemption proceeds and the remaining amount is paid to the investor.
The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) regulates the exit load structure and requirements for mutual funds in India.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker for facilitating transactions, such as buying or selling securities.
The fee is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat rate per transaction.
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of transaction, the broker, and the market or exchange.
These fees are usually deducted from the transaction amount or paid separately by the client.
Brokerage fees can apply to various financial instruments, including stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and commodities.
The fee structure and rates are often disclosed by the broker in their terms and conditions or on their website.

INDmoney is one of the best finance management apps I have used. It provides a clean and user-friendly interface to track investments, mutual funds, stocks, EPF, loans, and overall net worth in one place. The app makes it easy to monitor financial growth with detailed insights and reports. The U.S. stock investing feature is smooth, and account aggregation helps users get a complete picture of their finances. Notifications and reminders are useful without being overwhelming.
